Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

BagM 21, 483 [word-problems; diagram][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
o i 1o i 1

(o i 1) [...]

o i 22

[...]

o i 33

10 [...]-⸢ia⸣ 10 NINDA di-ik-šu₂ ša₂ ŠA₃ EN 20 A.RA₂ 10 3.20 MU 10 1/2

(o i 3) My [diameter] is 10. What is the extension of the middle?

(o i 3) 20 times 10 is 3 20. Because 10 is 1/2 of [20] add 7;30, 1/2 of 15, to 30, so that 37;30. 2;20 times 37;30 is 2 05. The area is 1 ikû 25 mušaru. The crescent is 30. What is the area? 30 times 30 is 15 00. Multiply <by> 0;05 50 (so that) 1 27;30. The 1st crescent is 1/2 (ikû) 37 1/2 mušaru area. Go times 2. Multiply 1 27;30 by 2 so that 2;55. The 2nd crescents are 1 1/2 (ikû) 25 mušaru area. Sum them so that they are all 3 (ikû) area.

o i 44

ša [20] 7.30 1/2 15 a-na 30 TAB-ma 37.30 2.20 A.RA₂

o i 55

37.30 2.05 1(IKU)gana₂ 25 SAR GANA₂-KAM 30 ša₂ gana₂U₄.SAKAR A.ŠA₃

o i 66

EN 30 A.RA₂ 30 15 5.50 DU 1.27.30 1/2(IKU)gana₂ 37 1/2 SAR

o i 77

1-KAM gana₂U₄.SAKAR A.RA₂ 2 DUik 1.27.30 A.RA₂ 2 DU-ma 2.55

o i 88

1(EŠE₃)gana₂ 25 SAR 2-KAM gana₂U₄.SAKAR-MEŠ GAR.GAR-šu₂-nu-ti-⸢ma DU₃⸣-šu₂-nu-ma 3(iku)gana₂

o i 99

1 gana₂GUR₂ 1 UŠ GUR₂up A.RA₂ 4 2 NINDA-AM₃ na-pal E₁₁.A

(o i 9) 1 figure of a circle. 1 curved 1 cable. I took out the digging-out (?) 4 times, 2 rods each. What are each of the areas?

o i 1010

EN-AM₃ A.ŠA₃ti-ME

o i 1111

[54 A].⸢RA₂⸣ 2 1.48 1(IKU)gana₂ 8 SAR na-pal KA₂-a-nu-u₂

(o i 11) [54 times] 2 is 1 48. The outer digging-out is 1 (ikû) 8 mušaru area. [42] times 2 is 1 24. The second digging-out is 1/2 (ikû) 34 mušaru area. [30] times 2 is 1 00. The third digging-out is 1/2 (ikû) 10 mušaru area. [18] times 2 is 36. The fourth digging-out is 36 mušaru. (area) [12] times 12 is 2 24. Multiply 2 24 by 0;05, so that 12. The fifth, inner digging-out is [12 mušaru (area)]. Sum them so that they are all 3 (ikû) area.

o i 1212

[42] ⸢A⸣.RA₂ 2 1.24 1/2(IKU)gana₂ 34 SAR na-pal 2u₂

o i 1313

[30] ⸢A⸣.RA₂ 2 1 1/2(IKU)gana₂ 10 SAR na-pal 3šu₂

o i 1414

[18] ⸢A⸣.RA₂ 2 36 36 SAR na-pal 4u₂

o i 1515

[12] ⸢A⸣.RA₂ 12 2.24 2.24 A.RA₂ 5 DU-ma 12

o i 1616

[12] ⸢SAR⸣ na-pal 5šu₂ E₂-a-nu-u₂ GAR.GAR-šu₂-nu-ti-ma DU₃-šu₂-nu 3(IKU)gana₂

o i 1717

[ma?-gar?]-ri 1/2 NINDA GUR₂up 4 ŠU.SI gišŠA₃.KAL 1 ŠU.SI nap-du-u₂

(o i 17) [A wheel (?)] curves 1/2 rod: the shakkullu wood is 4 fingers, the fastening band is 1 finger, the rim [3 fingers], the spokes (?) 17 fingers, the hub 1/3 cubit.

o i 1818

[3 ŠU.SI] li-mi-ti 17 ŠU.SI AL.ŠID 1/3 KUŠ₃ tu-lu-u₂

(rest of column missing)
Column ii
o ii 1o ii 1

⸢18⸣ 2/3 GIN₂ A.ŠA₃ gišŠA₃.KAL 4 GIN₂ IGI 4-GAL₂.[LA]

(o ii 1) [......] The area of the shakkullu wood is [1]8 2/3 shekels. The area of the fastening band is 4 shekels and a 4th. The area of the rim is [11] shekels and 3 4ths. The area of 1 spoke (?) is 6 and 2/3 shekels. The area of 6 spokes (?) is 1/2 mina 8 shekels and a 4th. The area of the hub is 2 shekels 15 grains. Sum them so that they are all 1 mušaru 15 shekels.

o ii 22

A.ŠA₃ nap-du-[u₂]

o ii 33

[11] GIN₂ 3 IGI 4-GAL₂.LA A.ŠA₃ li-mi-ti 6 2/3 ⸢GIN₂⸣

o ii 44

[A].ŠA₃ 1-KAM AL.ŠID 1/2 MA.NA 8 GIN₂ IGI 4-GAL₂.[LA]

o ii 55

A.ŠA₃ 6

o ii 66

AL.ŠID 2 GIN₂ 15 ŠE A.ŠA₃ tu-lu-[u₂]

o ii 77

GAR.GAR-šu₂-nu-ti-ma 1 SAR 15 GIN₂ DU₃-šu₂-nu

o ii 88

GI-MEŠ ša₂ GI 1 NINDA US₂ GI 1 NINDA SAG 1 SAR šum-⸢ma⸣

(o ii 8) Reeds, of length 1 rod(-reed), of width 1 rod(-reed) — (that is,) 1 mušaru (area).

(o ii 8) If your cubit is 0;05, <you multiply> the line times ditto and times [1]. <You take times 1 each.>

o ii 99

5 am-mat-ka mi-ḫi-il-tu₄ A.RA₂ KI.MIN<(mi-ḫi-il-ti)> u₃ A.RA₂ [1]

o ii 1010

25 DUak A.RA₂ 2.24-AM₃

(o ii 10) <If your cubit is 1> you multiply <times ditto and times> 25. <You take> times 2 24 each.

o ii 1111

⸢ṣu⸣-up-pan US₂ u₃ ṣu-up-pan SAG EN SAR-ME šum-ma

(o ii 11) The length is a chain, the width a chain. What are the mušaru (area)?

(o ii 11) If your cubit is 0;05, there are 5 chains. You multiply 5 by 5 so that 25. The mušaru are 25.

o ii 1212

⸢5⸣ am-mat-ka 5 ṣu-up-pan 5 A.RA₂ 5 DU-ma 25 25 SAR.SAR

o ii 1313

⸢šum⸣-ma 1 am-mat-ka 1 ṣu-up-pan 1 A.RA₂ 1 DUlak-ma [1]

(o ii 13) If your cubit is 1, there are 1 00 chains. You multiply 1 times 1 so that [1], You multiply 1 times 25, so that 25.

o ii 1414

⸢1⸣ A.RA₂ 25 DU-ma 25

o ii 1515

⸢E₂⸣ 25 SAR-MEŠ UR.A ḪE₂.EN šum-ma 5 am-mat-ka

(o ii 15) A house of 25 mušaru (area). What should the square-side be?

(o ii 15) If your cubit is 0;05, for each 1 you take 25. <5, a chain, is the square-side.>

o ii 1616

⸢1⸣-[E]-AM₃ 25 TIqe₂ šum-ma 1 am-mat-ka 25 A.RA₂

(o ii 16) If your cubit is 1, you multiply 25 by 2 24, so that you take 1 00 00 each. <1 00>, a chain, is the square-side.

o ii 1717

⸢2⸣.24 DU-ma 1-E-AM₃ TIqe₂ ṣu-up-pan UR.⸢A⸣

o ii 1818

⸢SAG⸣ 4 US₂ EN ḪE₂.GID₂.DA ḪE₂ 20 SAR.SAR šum-ma [5]

(o ii 18) [The width is] 4. What length should I survey so that it is 20 mušaru (area)?

(o ii 18) If your cubit is [0;05], a 4th is 0;15. You multiply 0;15 times 20, [so that 5]. You survey [5], a chain.

o ii 1919

⸢am⸣-mat-ka IGI 4-GAL₂.LA 15 15 A.RA₂ 20 DU-[ma 5]

o ii 2020

⸢5 ṣu⸣-up-pan GID₂ad šum-ma 1 am-mat-ka IGI ⸢48⸣-[GAL₂].LA 1.15

(o ii 20) If your cubit is 1, a 4[8th is] 0;01 15. You multiply 0;01 15 by 2 24, so that 3. [You multiply] 3 times [20, so that 1 00].

o ii 2121

1.15 A.RA₂ 2.24 DU-ma 3 3 A.RA₂ [20 DU-ma 1]

o ii 2222

⸢GI⸣-MEŠ ša 6 KUŠ₃ US₂ 6 KUŠ₃ SAG 1 GI šum-[ma 5]

(o ii 22) Reeds, of length 6 cubits, of width 6 cubits, (or) 1 reed.

(o ii 22) If your cubit is [0;05], [you multiply] the line times ditto and times [2]. Times 0;30 square-side.

o ii 2323

⸢am-mat⸣-ka mi-⸢ḫi⸣-il-tu₂ A.RA₂ KI.MIN<(mi-ḫi-il-ti)> u₃ 2 ⸢tal⸣-[lak]

o ii 2424

A.RA₂ 30 IB.SA šum-ma 1 am-mat-ka mi-ḫi-⸢il⸣-[tu₂]

(o ii 24) If your cubit is 1, you multiply the line times [ditto] and <times> 50. Times 1 12 square-side.

o ii 2525

A.[RA₂ KI.MIN<(mi-ḫi-il-ti])> ⸢u₃⸣ 50 tal-⸢lak⸣ A.RA₂ 1.12 IB.[SA]

o ii 2626

[2 1/2 US₂ 2 1/2] SAG ḪE₂.EN ⸢GI⸣-MEŠ šum-⸢ma⸣

(o ii 26) [The length is 2 1/2 rods], the width [2 1/2 rods]. What should the reeds be?

(o ii 26) If [your cubit is 0;05], you multiply 2;30 times 2;30, so that 6;15. [You multiply 6;15 times 2], so that 12;30. The reeds are 25.

o ii 2727

[5 am-mat-ka] 2.30 A.RA₂ 2.30 DU-ma 6.15

o ii 2828

[6.15 A.RA₂ 2] DU-ma 12.30 25 GI-⸢MEŠ⸣

o ii 2929

[šum-ma 1 am-mat]-ka 30-AM₃ 30 A.RA₂ 30 DU-ma ⸢15⸣

(o ii 29) [If] your [cubit is 1], 30 each. You multiply 30 times 30, so that 15 00. You multiply [15 00 times 0;00 50], so that 12;30.

o ii 3030

[15 A.RA₂ 15] ⸢DU⸣-ma 12.[30]

o ii 3131

[2 SAG EN ḪE₂].⸢GID₂⸣.DA!(MA) ḪE₂ GI-MEŠ MU la₃ [ZUu₂]

(o ii 31) [The width is 2. What length] should I survey so that it is 20 reeds?

(o ii 31) Because you do not [understand], raise [......], you multiply times the reciprocal of the coefficient [of ...] area (?) and times the reeds, so that you will see.

o ii 3232

[...] IL₂-ma A.RA₂ IGIe ša IGI.GUB [...]

o ii 3333

[...] ⸢u₃⸣ A.RA₂ GI-MEŠ DU-ma ⸢IGI.GUB⸣ [...]

o ii 3434

[šum-ma 5 am]-mat-ka 2 2 NINDA IGI 2 30 30 A.⸢RA₂⸣ 30 DU-[ma]

(o ii 34) If your cubit is [0;05], 2 rods is 2. The reciprocal of 2 is 0;30. You multiply 0;30 times 0;30, [so that 0;15]. You multiply [0;15 times] 10, so that 2 30. You survey 2 1/2 rods.

o ii 3535

[15 15 A].⸢RA₂⸣ 10 DU-ma 2.30!(20) 1 1/2 NINDA ta-ša-⸢ad⸣-da-⸢ad⸣

o ii 3636

[šum-ma 1 am-mat]-ka 24 2 NINDA IGI 24-GAL₂.LA

(o ii 36) [If] your [cubit is 1], 2 rods is 24. A 24th is [0;02 30]. You multiply [0;02 30] times 1 12, so that 3. [You multiply] 3 times [10, so that] 30. You survey 2 1/2 rods.

o ii 3737

[2.30 2.30] ⸢A⸣.RA₂ 1.12 DU-ma 3 3 A.RA₂

o ii 3838

[10 DU-ma] 30 2.30 NINDA ta-ša-ad-da-⸢ad⸣

o ii 3939

⸢E₂ UŠ-AM⸣ x x BAD₃tu₂ ḪE₂ 1 ME GI-⸢MEŠ⸣

(o ii 39) A house ... so that it is 1 hundred reeds?

o ii 4040

⸢MU⸣ [la₃] ZUu₂ GI-[MEŠ] ⸢A⸣.RA₂ IGIe ša ⸢IGI.GUB⸣ [...]

(o ii 40) Because you do not understand, you multiply reeds times the reciprocal of the constant [of] reeds, and then [you take] each (square-side).

o ii 4141

GI-MEŠ DU-ma ⸢AM₃⸣ [TIqe₂] ⸢šum⸣-ma ⸢5⸣ [am-mat-ka]

(o ii 41) If [your cubit] is 0;05, you multiply 50 times 0;30, so that [25. You take each (square-side)]. The square-side [is a chain].
If [your] cubit is 1, [you multiply 50 times 1 12, so that] 2. ... You take each (square-side). [The square-side is a chain.]

o ii 4242

50 A.RA₂ 30 ⸢DU-ma⸣ [25 AM₃ TIqe₂ ṣu-up-pan]

o ii 4343

UR.⸢A šum⸣-ma 1 am-mat-[ka 50 A.RA₂ 1.12 DU-ma]

o ii 4444

2 AŠA₅ AM₃ TI⸢qe₂⸣ [ṣu-up-pan UR.A]

PA₅ ip-ṣi-ma a-⸢ta⸣-[...]

(o ii 45) (Too fragmentary to translate)

x mi-ḫi-<il>-tu₄ te-er-[...]

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

[...]

r i 22

16 16 x A 16 GI-MEŠ E₂ [...]

r i 33

GAR.GAR-šu₂-nu-ti-ma 20 GI-⸢MEŠ⸣ [...]

r i 44

1 ŠU KUŠ₃ ⸢UR.A⸣ E₂ 3 KUŠ₃ UR.A a-⸢na⸣ [1/2 GIN₂ a-ša₂-am]

(r i 4) The square-side of a house is sixty cubits. [I bought] a square-side of 3 cubits for [1/2 shekel]. What did I buy the total house for?

r i 55

ŠU.NIGIN₂ E₂ ⸢a⸣-na EN a-ša₂-mu MU la₃ [ZUu₂ ...]

(r i 5) Since [you] do not [understand, ...] you multiply the line which is sixty cubits [times ditto, times the reciprocal of ] the line [which is] 3 cubits times [ditto and times 30], so that you see.

r i 66

mi-ḫi-⸢il-tu₂⸣ ša₂ TA 1 ŠU KUŠ₃ ⸢A⸣.[RA₂ KI.MIN<(mi-ḫi-il-ti)> A.RA₂ IGIe ša₂]

r i 77

mi-ḫi-⸢il⸣-tu₄ ša 3 KUŠ₃ ⸢A⸣.[RA₂ KI.MIN<(mi-ḫi-il-ti)> u₃ A.RA₂ 30]

r i 88

DU-ma IGImar šum-ma am-mat-ka 5.[1 ŠU KUŠ₃ 15]

(r i 8) If your cubit <is 0;05>, [the sixty cubits] are 5, the 3 cubits [are 0;15]. [5] times 5 is 25. [You multiply] 0;15 <times> «you multiply» [0;15, so that 0;03 45]. the reciprocal of 0;03 45 is 16. 16 times 25 is 6 40. You multiply 6 40 by 0;30, [so that] 3 20.

r i 99

3 KUŠ₃ [5] A.RA₂ 5 25 ⸢15⸣ <A>.RA₂-<<ma>> 15 [DU-ma 3.45]

r i 1010

IGI 3.45 16 16 A.RA₂ 25 6.40 [6.40 A.RA₂]

r i 1111

30 DU-⸢ma⸣ 3.20 šum-ma 1 am-mat-ka [1 1 ŠU KUŠ₃]

(r i 11) If your cubit is 1 [the sixty cubits are 1 00], the 3 cubits are 3. 1 00 times <1 00 is 1 00 00> «3». 3 times 3 is 9. [A 9th is 0;06 40.] 0;06 40 times 1 00 00 is 6 40. You multiply 6 40 times 0;30, so that 3 20. You buy the total house for 3 1/3 mina.

r i 1212

3 3 KUŠ₃ 1 A.RA₂ 3 3 <3> A.RA₂ 3 9 [IGI 9-GAL₂.LA 6.40]

r i 1313

6.40 A.RA₂ 1.06.40 6.40 A.RA₂ 30 DU-ma [3.20 ...]

r i 1414

ŠU.NIGIN₂ E₂ a-na 3 1/3 MA.NA ta-ša-[am]

r i 1515

KI.MIN<(E₂)> 1 ŠU KUŠ₃ UR.A a-na 3 1/3!(2/3) MA.NA <a-ša₂-am> 3 ⸢KUŠ₃⸣ [UR.A]

(r i 15) Ditto. <I bought> a square-side of sixty cubits for 3 <1/3> «2/3» mina. What <did I buy> [a square-side of] 3 cubits for?

r i 1616

a-na EN MU la₃ ZUu₂ A.RA₂ mi-ḫi-il-[ti ša 3 KUŠ₃]

(r i 16) Because you do not understand, you multiply times the line [of 3 cubits] times the reciprocal of times the line of [sixty cubits], and you multiply times 3 <1/3> «2/3» mina, so that [you see].

r i 1717

A.RA₂ IGIe ša₂ A.RA₂ mi-ḫi-il-tu₂ ša [1 ŠU KUŠ₃]

r i 1818

⸢tal-lak⸣ u₃ ⸢A⸣.RA₂ 3 1/3!(2/3) MA.NA DU-⸢ma⸣ [IGImar]

r i 1919

⸢šum-ma 5⸣ [am]-⸢mat⸣-ka IGI 25-GAL₂.BI 2.24 [2.24]

(r i 19) If your [cubit] is 0;05, a 25th is 0;02 24. [0;02 24] times 0;03 45 is 0;00 09. You multiply 0;00 09 times 3 20, so that 0;30.

r i 2020

A.RA₂ 3.⸢45⸣ 9 9 A.RA₂ 3.20 DU-ma [30 šum-ma]

(r i 20) [If] your cubit is 1, a 1 00 00th is 0;00 01. [You multiply] 0;00 01 times 9, [so that 0;00 09]. You multiply [0;00 09] times 3 20, so that 0;30. <You buy> a square-side of 3 cubits for 1/2 [shekel] silver.

r i 2121

1 am-mat-⸢ka⸣ IGI 1-GAL₂.BI 1 1 A.RA₂ 9 [DU-ma 9 9 A.RA₂]

r i 2222

3.20 DU-ma 30 3 KUŠ₃ UR.A a-na 1/2 [GIN₂ KU₃].⸢BABBAR⸣

r i 2323

KI.MIN<(E₂)> a-na [3] 1/3!(2/3) MA.NA ⸢KU₃⸣.BABBAR ša₂ 1 ŠU ⸢KUŠ₃ UR⸣.A

(r i 23) Ditto. For [3] <1/3> «2/3» mina silver I bought a square-side of 1 sixty cubits. What square-side could I buy for 1/2 shekel of silver?

r i 2424

a-ša₂-mu a-⸢na⸣ 1/2 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR EN UR.⸢A⸣ ḪE₂.SA₁₀

r i 2525

MU la₃ ⸢ZU⸣u₂ IGIu₂ ša₂ 3 1/3!(5/6) MA.NA A.⸢RA₂⸣ 30 DU-ma

(r i 25) Because you do not understand, you multiply the reciprocal of 3 <1/3> «5/6» times 0;30, and then you multiply that which came up for you to see times the line [of sixty] cubits, and then you take each (square-side). The reciprocal of 3 20 is 0;00 18. You multiply times 0;30, so that 0;00 09.

r i 2626

a-na IGI-ka E₁₁.A A.RA₂ mi-ḫi-⸢il⸣-tu₄

r i 2727

[ša₂ 1 ŠU] ⸢KUŠ₃⸣ DU-ma AM₃ TIqe₂ IGI 3.20 18

r i 2828

A.⸢RA₂⸣ 30 DU-ma 9 šum-ma 5 am-mat-⸢ka⸣ 9

(r i 28) If your cubit is 0;05, you multiply 0;00 09 by 25, so that 0;03 45. You take 0;15, each (square-side).

r i 2929

A.RA₂ 25 DU-ma 3.45-E 15-⸢AM₃⸣ TIqe₂

r i 3030

⸢šum⸣-ma 1 am-mat-ka 9 A.RA₂ 1 9-⸢E⸣ 3-⸢AM₃⸣ <TIqe₂>

(r i 30) If your cubit is 1, 0;00 09 times 1 00 00 is 9. <You take> 3, each <square-side>. For 1/2 shekel silver <you buy> a square-side of 3 cubits each.

r i 3131

a-na 1/2 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR 3 KUŠ₃ [UR].A-⸢AM₃⸣

r i 3232

KI.MIN<(E₂)> a-na 1/2 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR 3 KUŠ₃ UR.[A] a-ša₂-am

(r i 32) Ditto. For 1/2 shekel of silver I bought a square-side of 3 cubits. What square-side could I buy for 3 <1/3> «5/6» mina of silver?

r i 3333

a-na 3 1/3!(5/6) MA.NA EN UR.A IB₂.SA₁₀

r i 3434

MU la₃ ZUu₂ IGIu₂ ša 1/2 GIN₂ A.RA₂ 3 1/3!(5/6) MA.NA

(r i 34) Because you do not understand, you multiply the reciprocal of 1/2 shekel times 3 <1/3> «5/6» mina, and then you take each <square-side>, and then you multiply times 3 cubits, so that you see. A 30th is 2. You multiply by 3 20, and then you take 20, each (square-side) of 6 40.

r i 3535

DU-ma AM₃ TIqe₂-ma A.RA₂ 3 KUŠ₃ DU-ma

r i 3636

IGImar IGI 30-GAL₂.BI 2 A.RA₂ 3.20 DU-ma

r i 3737

6.40-E 20-AM₃ TIqe₂ šum-ma <5> am-mat-ka

(r i 37) If your cubit is <0;05>, you multiply 20 times 0;15, so that the square-side of the house is 5, a chain.

r i 3838

20 A.RA₂ 15 DU-ma 5 ṣu-up-pan UR.A E₂

r i 3939

šum-ma 1 am-mat-ka 20 A.RA₂ 3 DU-ma 1 ŠU KUŠ₃

(r i 39) If your cubit is 1, you multiply 20 by 3, so that 1 00. The square-side of the house is sixty cubits.

r i 4040

mit-ḫar-ti E₂

r i 4141

20 KUŠ₃ UR.A TUR₃ 2/3!(5/6) KUŠ₃ UR.A a-gur-ru tar-<ki>-be₂-e

(r i 41) The square-side of a courtyard is 20 cubits. The square-side of a baked brick for paving is <2/3> «5/6» cubit. What is the number of bricks that are in the courtyard?

r i 4242

EN mi-nu-ut a-gur-ru ša₂ qe₂-reb TUR₃ MU la₃ ZUu₂

(r i 42) Because you do not know, you multiply the constant of line of the courtyard times the reciprocal of the constant of the line of the baked brick, so that you see the number of the bricks.

r i 4343

IGI.GUB mi-ḫi-il-tu₂ ša₂ TUR₃ A.RA₂ IGIe ša₂ IGI!(A)GUB

r i 4444

⸢mi⸣-ḫi-il-tu₄ ša₂ a-gur-ru DU-ma mi-nu-ut a-gur-ru ⸢IGI⸣[mar]

r i 4545

šum-ma 5 am-mat-ka 1.40 A.RA₂ 1.40 2.46.40 ⸢3⸣.[20 A].RA₂

(r i 45) If your cubit is 0;05, 1;40 times 1 ;40 is 2;46 40. You multiply [0;03 20] times 0;03 20, so that 0;00 <1>1 16 40. The reciprocal of 0;00 11 16 40 is 5 24. You multiply 5 24 [times] 2;46 40, so that 15 00.

r i 4646

⸢3⸣.20 DU-ma 11!(01)16.40 IGI 11.16.40 5.24 5.24 [A.RA₂]

r i 4747

⸢2⸣.46.40 DU-ma 15 šum-ma 1 am-mat-ka 20 A.⸢RA₂⸣ [20 6.40]

(r i 47) If your cubit is 1, 20 times [20 is 6 40. 0;40] times 0;40 is 0;26 40. The reciprocal of 0;26 40 is 2;15. [You multiply] 2;15 [times 6 40], so that 15 00, (or) 9 hundred. There are 9 hundred baked bricks.

r i 4848

[40] A.RA₂ 40 26.40 IGI 26 :. 40 2.15 2.15 [A.RA₂ 6.40]

r i 4949

[DU]-⸢ma⸣ 15 9 ME 9 ME a-gur-⸢ru⸣

Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

[...] 3 KUŠ₃ UR.A

(r ii 1) [...] 3 cubits square-side [...] breadth [...] livestock [...] multiply, so that the total sheepfold is 1 40.

r ii 22

[...] DAGAL

r ii 33

[...] MAŠ₂.ANŠE

r ii 44

[...] ⸢DU-ma⸣ 1.40 ŠU.NIGIN₂ AMAŠ

r ii 55

[10 A.RA₂ 10 1.40 1(IKU)gana₂] 7.30 A.RA₂ 7.30 56.15

(r ii 5) [10 times 10 is 1 40, (or) 1 ikû.] 7;30 times 7;30 is 56;15, (or) [56 mušaru, 15 shekels. 5 times 5 is 25, (or) 25 mušaru]. 2;30 times 2;30 is 6;15, (or) [6 mušaru 15 shekels]. [6;15 times 30 is 3] 07;30. The reciprocal of 3 07;30 is 0;00 19 12. 0;00 19 12 [times 1 40 is 0;32 ...]. 3x600 lengths, square-side of the livestock (??).

r ii 66

[56 SAR 15 GIN₂ 5 A.RA₂ 5 25 25] ⸢SAR!⸣ 2.30 A.RA₂ 2.30 6.15

r ii 77

[6 SAR 15 GIN₂ 6.15 A.RA₂ 30 3].⸢07⸣.30 IGI 3.07.30 19.12 19.12

r ii 88

[A.RA₂ 1.40 32 ...] 3(GEŠʾU) UŠ US₂ UR.A MAŠ₂.ANŠE

r ii 99

[32 A.RA₂ 1.40] 53.20 1/2(IKU)gana₂ 3 1/3!(2/3) SAR A.ŠA₃

(r ii 9) [0;32 times 1 40] is 53;20. The [...] area is 1/2 ikû 3 <1/3> «2/3» mušaru. You multiply 0;32 times 56;15, so that 30. [The ... area is] 30 mušaru. You multiply [0;32] times 25, so that 13;20. [The ... area] is 13 1/3 mušaru. You multiply [0;32] times 6;15, so that 3;20. [The ... area] is 3 1/3 mušaru. Sum them all, so that the total courtyard is 1 40, (or) 1 ikû.

r ii 1010

[... 32] A.RA₂ 56.15 DU-ma 30 30 SAR

r ii 1111

[A.ŠA₃ ... 32] A.RA₂ 25 DU-ma 13.20 13 1/3 SAR

r ii 1212

[A.ŠA₃ ... 32] A.RA₂ 6.15 DU-ma 3.20 3 1/2 SAR

r ii 1313

[A.ŠA₃ ... GAR].GAR-šu₂-nu-ti-ma 1.40 1(IKU)gana₂ ŠU.NIGIN₂ TUR₃

r ii 1414

[GI-MEŠ ša₂] 7 KUŠ₃ US₂ 7 KUŠ₃ SAG 1 GI ša TUR qe₂-reb

(r ii 14) [Reed measure, of which] the length is 7 cubits, the width 7 cubits, is 1 reed (area), by the small cubit (?). You multiply the line times ditto and 1 12, and then you double that which came up for you to see [...] number of reeds and small cubits. The square-side is 1 10 cubits. What should the reed measure be?

r ii 1515

[mi-ḫi]-⸢il⸣-tu₄ A.RA₂ KI.MIN<(mi-ḫi-il-ti)> u₃ 1.12 DU-ma ša₂ a-na IGI-ka E₁₁.A

r ii 1616

⸢x⸣ mi-nu-ti GI-MEŠ KUŠ₃-MEŠ UR-MEŠ te-eṣ-ṣip 1.10 KUŠ₃ UR.A

r ii 1717

⸢ḪE₂⸣.EN GI-MEŠ 1.10 A.RA₂ 1.10 1.21.40 1.21.40 A.RA₂ 1.12 <1.38>

(r ii 17) 1 10 times 1 10 is 1 21 40. 1 21 40 times 0;01 12 (is 1 38). 1 38 reeds and 1 38 small cubits, or 2 reeds. You double 2 reeds to 1 38, so that the reeds are 1 hundred.

r ii 1818

1.38 GI-MEŠ 1.38 KUŠ₃-MEŠ TUR-MEŠ 2 GI-MEŠ 2 GI-MEŠ a-na

r ii 1919

1.38 te-eṣ-ṣip-ma 1 ME GI-MEŠ

r ii 2020

1 GI A.RA₂ 1 GI 1 GI 1 GI A.RA₂ 1 KUŠ₃ 1 KUŠ₃

(r ii 20) 1 reed times 1 reed is 1 reed. 1 reed times 1 cubit is 1 cubit. 1 reed times 1 finger is 1 finger. 1 cubit times 1 reed is 1 cubit. [1] cubit times 1 cubit is 1 small cubit. 1 cubit times 1 finger is 1 grain.

r ii 2121

1 GI A.RA₂ 1 ŠU.SI 1 ŠU.SI 1 KUŠ₃ A.RA₂ 1 GI 1 KUŠ₃

r ii 2222

[1] KUŠ₃ A.RA₂ 1 KUŠ₃ 1 KUŠ₃ TURtu₂ 1 KUŠ₃ A.RA₂ 1 ŠU.SI 1 ⸢ŠE⸣

r ii 2323

[1] ⸢ŠU⸣.SI <A.RA₂> 1 GI 1 ŠU.SI 1 ŠU.SI A.RA₂ 1 KUŠ₃ 1 [ŠE]

(r ii 23) [1] finger <times> 1 reed is 1 finger. 1 finger times 1 cubit is 1 [grain]. [1] finger times <1 finger is> 1 small finger. 24 small fingers are [1] grain. 7 grains are 1 finger. 24 fingers are 1 cubit. 7 cubits are 1 reed. 3 fingers and 3 grains are 1 small cubit. 7 small cubits are 1 cubit.

r ii 2424

[1 ŠU].SI A.RA₂ <1 ŠU.SI> 1 ŠU.SI TURtu₂ 24 ŠU.SI-⸢MEŠ⸣ TUR-MEŠ

r ii 2525

[1] ŠE 7 ŠE-MEŠ 1 ŠU.SI 24 ŠU.SI-MEŠ 1 ⸢KUŠ₃⸣ 7 KUŠ₃-MEŠ 1 GI

r ii 2626

3 ŠU.SI 3 ŠE 1 KUŠ₃ TURtu₂ 7 KUŠ₃ ⸢TUR⸣-MEŠ 1 KUŠ₃

r ii 2727

3 SILA₃ ŠE.NUMUN ḪE₂.EN GI-MEŠ MU ⸢la₃ ZU⸣u₂ IGIu₂

(r ii 27) 3 qû in seed-measure. What should the reed-measure be?

(r ii 27) Because you do not understand, you multiply the reciprocal of the coefficient of seed-measure times the coefficient of reed-measure and times the seed-measure, so that [you see] the reed-measure.

r ii 2828

ša₂ IGI.GUBbe₂-e ŠE.NUMUN

r ii 2929

⸢A.RA₂ IGI.GUB⸣u₂ ša₂ GI-MEŠ ⸢tal-lak⸣ u₃ A.RA₂ ŠE.NUMUN DU-ma

r ii 3030

GI-⸢MEŠ⸣ [IGImar IGI] ⸢48-GAL₂⸣.BI 1.15 1.15 A.RA₂ 2 2.30 2.30

(r ii 30) [The reciprocal of] 48 is 0;01 15. 0;01 15 times 2 is 0;02 30. 0;02 30 times 5 is 0;12 30. The reed-measure is 25. Ditto. The reciprocal of 0;20 is 3. You multiply 3 times 50, so that 2 30.

r ii 3131

A.RA₂ ⸢5⸣ ⸢12.30 25⸣ GI-MEŠ KI.MIN IGI 20-GAL₂.BI 3 3

r ii 3232

A.RA₂ 50 ⸢DU⸣-ma 2.30

r ii 3333

⸢25 GI-MEŠ ḪE₂⸣.EN ŠE.NUMUN MU la₃ ZUu₂ IGIu₂ ša₂ IGI.GUBbe₂-e

(r ii 33) 25 reeds. What should the seed-measure be?

(r ii 33) Because you do not understand, you multiply the reciprocal of the coefficient of reed-measure times the coefficient of seed-measure, and you multiply times the reeds, so that you see the seed-measure.

r ii 3434

⸢GI⸣-MEŠ A.RA₂ IGI.GUBu₂ ša₂ ŠE.NUMUN DUak u₃ A.RA₂ GI-MEŠ

r ii 3535

DU-ma ŠE.NUMUN tam-mar IGI 2 30 30 A.RA₂ 48 24

(r ii 35) The reciprocal of 2 is 0;30. 0;30 times 0;00 48 is 0;00 24. You multiply 0;00 24 times 12;30 so that 0;05. The seed-measure is 3 qû. Ditto. The reciprocal of 50 is 0;01 12. 0;01 12 times <0;20> is 0;00 24.

r ii 3636

24 A.RA₂ 12.30 DU-ma 5 3 SILA₃ ŠE.NUMUN

r ii 3737

KI.MIN IGI 50-GAL₂.BI 1.12 1.12 A.RA₂ <20> 24

r ii 3838

9 ME a-gur-ru ša₂ 2/3!(5/6) KUŠ₃-AM₃ TUR₃ u₂-rab-bi EN UR.A

(r ii 38) I enlarged a courtyard with 9 hundred baked bricks of <2/3> «5/6» cubits each (square-side). What is the square-side of the courtyard?

r ii 3939

TUR₃ AM₃ ša₂ a-gur-ru A.RA₂ 40 DUak 15-E 30-AM₃

(r ii 39) You multiply each (square-side) of the baked bricks times 0;40. You take 30, each (square-side) of 15 00. You multiply 30 times 0;40, so that 20. The square-side of the courtyard is 20 cubits.

r ii 4040

TIqe₂ 30 A.RA₂ 40 DU-ma 20 20 KUŠ₃ UR.A TUR₃

(colophon)
r ii 4141

ŠE.NUMUN u GI-MEŠ AL.TIL

(r ii 41) Seed-measure and reed-measure, finished. Copy of a wooden writing-board, written and collated according to its original. Tablet of Šamaš-iddina, son of Nadinu, descendant of Šangû-Ninurta, incantation-priest, Urukian.

r ii 4242

GABA.RI gišDA LIBIR.RA.BI.GIM AB.SAR BA.AN.E₃

r ii 4343

ṭup-pi mdUTU—SUMna DUMU-A.NI ša

r ii 4444

mna-di-nu DUMU lu₂E₂.MAŠ—dNIN.URTA

r ii 4545

lu₂⸢MAŠ⸣.MAŠ LU₂ qaq-qar da-nuki-u₂

(traces)
